Deus Ex: Human Revolution - Director's Cut

notes:
Minor issues across the board, but playable from start-to-finish without any dramas. The game stutters when entering loading zones and briefly at the start of levels, but after that the performance is more-or-less comparable to native; disabling Esync and running in DX9 with no tesselation can help mitigate some of the stutter.

There are minor visual glitches with one particular texture in Hengsha (a framed picture), but it's entirely possible that issue is with the game itself and not caused by Proton. Similarly, I suspect unless you disable desktop composition while playing, enabling vsync causes mild screen-tearing localized at the top of the screen, but it's barely noticeable in gameplay.

Controller support out-of-the-box, no problems there.
title: Deus Ex: Human Revolution - Director's Cut

notes: Game runs very well out of the box with highest settings in DX11 mode. Lowering the settings, e. g. DX9, introduced massive stutter somehow?! Closing the game can last a while (up to a minute). The game may start to stutter after several hours of gameplay, which might be a memory leak issue with the game itself. Restarting the game solves this issue.
title: Deus Ex: Human Revolution - Director's Cut

notes: Esync disabled or audio produces artifacts. Some users may need to disable time based scheduling in pulseaudio. Game stutters sometimes when first rendering areas and npcs and when accessing camera feeds but it only lasts a moment. Shadows must be set to normal or they present artifacts, tessellation must be off. I run FXAA Medium with v-sync on. Runs perfectly with great performance on DX11 otherwise.

notes:
Sound is pitched lower than it should be, possibly a sampling issue but disabling esync didn't make a difference.  The Witcher3 also has this issue when running under Proton 4.2

The game has unbearable stuttering issues when panning the view around an environment.   This might mean there is an issue with shader caching or loading textures.   Again, TW3 has the same issue.

Hardware is most certainly not the problem - both titles are more than performant under bare-metal windows (yuck) and several other titles have no problems.

title: Deus Ex: Human Revolution - Director's Cut

notes:
Esync (and Fsync, if using a kernel with Valve's patch) are the source of stuttering in this game.  Game appears to hang on exit, but will eventually do so after a minute or two.  Launch options:  
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1,
PROTON_NO_FSYNC=1 (if using Proton 4.11-1 with a patched kernel),
PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 (optional with Proton 4.11-1).  Game seems fine with default DXVK which will only work if DX11 is turned on in the game, and it is restarted.  If game options are left at DX9 but PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 is not set, game may default to slower WINE OpenGL.
title: Deus Ex: Human Revolution - Director's Cut

notes: Game runs pretty well on high settings. No lag and only had one crash. Really smooth experience so far. Set the launch options with "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1, PROTON_NO_FSYNC=1, PROTON_USE_D9VK=1"
